fact_verdicts.note: Action metadata block states action id b3d452bff7769d7f557ec6b8974760ee6c5e496c276652b654032966621e0ccf#8, type TreasuryWithdrawals, treasury amount 540750000000 lovelace, proposed epoch 638, expires after 645. All five values match exactly.
Anchor body.onChain.gov_action.rewards contains key stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v with value "540750000000". Exact match.
Anchor body.title reads "Withdraw 540,750 ada for Pallas by TxPipe: Maintaining Cardano's Core Rust Li..." verbatim, truncation included.
Motivation: "requests 420,000 ADA (plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve) to fund **a part-time maintainer for Pallas**... over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD". All figures match.

Rationale Budget Summary table: WP1 - Pallas Maintenance and Enhancement 525,000; Intersect Budget Administration fee 15,750; Total 540,750. Exact match, and the total reconciles to the on-chain lovelace amount.

Motivation: "over 649 pull requests, 199 stars, 91 forks, 60 contributors, and 214,446 downloads on crates.io". All five figures match. (These are proposer self-reported claims, not independently verified by the source material, but the fact is stated as what the proposal says.)

Motivation names Aiken, Dolos, Lucid, Oura, Mithril, Amaru "and many others"; the rationale's Strategic Pillar Alignment section repeats that list and adds UTxO-RPC. The fact's attribution to both sections is correct.

Rationale, Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure: "TxPipe has received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals across Funds 9 through 14, of which 26 have been successfully delivered and 4 are currently under development and on schedule." Exact match.

Rationale, Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure: "TxPipe received open-source maintenance funding through Intersect's 2025 budget cycle for Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC, all of which are being delivered on schedule." Exact match.

Rationale, Net Change Limit Compliance: "The requested amount does not at time of submission, on its own or in aggregate, breach the applicable 350M Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713." Cap size and both epoch bounds match.

values_hierarchy.md ranks (2) Treasury stewardship and downside protection above (3) Evidence quality and reproducibility and (4) Public-benefit ecosystem growth. The stated ordering is directly entailed.

treasury_spending_doctrine.md, Dossier Gate Posture (amended 2026-07-10, v1.5.0): "**Hard gate (current):** incomplete independently verified diligence means `NEEDS_MORE_INFO`, never an automatic NO." Exact match including the "current" designation.

verification.summary: All 15 dossier facts are supported by the supplied source material; none are contradicted and none contain invented specifics. Facts 1-2 match the on-chain action header and the anchor's onChain.gov_action.rewards block exactly. Facts 3-9 and 13 reproduce the anchor's title, motivation, budget table, adoption metrics, dependent-project list, prior-funding disclosure, and NCL-compliance statement with all figures and named entities matching verbatim. Facts 10-12 match the on-chain context block digit-for-digit (recipient history, explicit DRep ballot counts, 36-epoch treasury flow). Facts 14-15 correctly restate the doctrine's values ordering and the current hard-gate posture. Money figures are also internally consistent across sources: the motivation's 420,000 ADA plus 105,000 ADA contingency equals the 525,000 ADA WP1 line, which plus the 15,750 ADA Intersect administration fee equals the 540,750 ADA total, matching the 540,750,000,000 lovelace withdrawal in both the action header and the anchor. Note for the decision-maker (not a discrepancy, since the facts are framed as what the proposal asserts): the adoption metrics in fact 6, the Catalyst delivery record in fact 8, the on-schedule claim in fact 9, and the 67% Hydra voting threshold cited in the anchor are all proposer self-reported and are not independently corroborated anywhere in the supplied material.
